title: European car sales rise modestly in February, Tesla reverses skid
description: STORY: New car sales in Europe rose in February after falling in January.And sustained demand for electric vehicles helped Tesla achieve growth for the first time since 2024.Data from the European auto lobby the ACEA showed overall car registrations, a proxy for sales, in the EU, Britain and the European Free Trade Association were up 1.7%.Over 979,000 cars were sold in the month, with two-thirds of those being electric or hybrid. The EU and Britain are walking back some regulations aimed at reducing CO2 emissions.That's after pressure from domestic carmakers who say they are struggling to turn a profit on EV sales...while facing competition from Chinese rivals.But battery-electric and plug-in hybrid sales have been steadily on the rise in Europe thanks to new, cheaper models.The data showed Tesla's February registrations were up 11.8% year-on-year ...They were marginally lower than those of its Chinese competitor BYD, whose sales more than doubled from the same month of 2025.Sales of top domestic carmakers VW and Stellantis rose by 2.2% and 9.5%, respectively.While Renault's fell by 14.3%.

title: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
description: Zoox, Amazon's self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi ‌service in San Francisco and Las Vegas, ‌and begin testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking ​its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing market.  Alphabet's Waymo leads the robotaxi market, while Tesla has the financial resources and manufacturing scale to ‌expand its limited service ⁠in Austin, Texas, and plans to ramp up rapidly across the United States ⁠this year.  Meanwhile, Zoox plans to significantly grow its footprint in San Francisco, focusing on dense, high-demand neighborhoods ​such as ​the Marina, Chinatown and ​the Embarcadero, while expanding ‌in Las Vegas to cover more hotels and entertainment destinations along the Strip.
